summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer ships a crypto-clipboard hijacker with system persistence
details: This package is a typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer (1-character + suffix edit of the scope and name) whose README is copied verbatim from the legitimate package — it even instructs users to `import from '@juggle/resize-observer'`. The shipped code is unrelated to a ResizeObserver polyfill. On `npm install`, the postinstall hook runs `npm-install.js`, which probes for any available `pip` and installs a bundled Python package `clipboard_guardian` system-wide using `pip install --upgrade --break-system-packages`, bypassing PEP 668. On Linux it additionally attempts `sudo apt-get/pacman/dnf install xclip`. After install, it writes persistence with deliberately OS-impersonating names: a systemd user unit `python3-dbus-helper.service`, a macOS LaunchAgent `com.apple.python.runtime.plist`, or a Windows scheduled task `PyRuntimeBroker` triggered AtLogOn, each configured to keep the helper running. The Python helper (`guardian.py`) polls the clipboard every 0.4s, regex-matches wallet addresses for ~40 chains (BTC, ETH, XMR, SOL, TRX, TON, etc.), and silently overwrites them with hardcoded attacker-owned addresses (e.g. ETH `0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18`, BTC `bc1qs2mpls4p0f7fng073gy2rcdgjpf7la4eugpt6y`). It renames its own process to match the cover-story service name and pauses when system monitors (htop, Task Manager, Activity Monitor) are running to evade detection. Any user who later copies a wallet address to send cryptocurrency will have the destination silently swapped to the attacker's wallet.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
